section_title: THE RANDOMNESS OF WEEKEND OPENING PRICES
content:
Now for some of you and and these are the folks that like to just the argumentative on the weekend, okay.
When I do commentary on a Friday or say it's like on a Saturday when the Market's closed, I don't have a
skill, I don't have a repertoire or a mechanism, a gizmo, a procedure to be able to forecast what the Sunday
opening price is going to be, okay. Nobody's going to have that. That is always a random function of
wherever they want to open the market up. And when I say 'they', the collective hand that makes these
markets, okay. So there's no rhyme or reason as to where we can forecast an opening price, okay, with any
consistency, something that could be derived as a model.

section_title: MANAGING GAP RISK & INTRADAY TRADING FOCUS
content:
In the past I have taken trades which I won't do anymore because of the Gap risk that's inherent now with
all the things that's going on around the world, new potential War, you paying attention to the things around
the world that may cause violence in your neck of the woods? So because of that I'm not willing to have
any positions over the weekend. And I'm more inclined to just stay inside of intraday trading so I'm in and
out, take surgical strikes, move to the sidelines, not even trying to trade, you know, overnight into the next
day. Very rarely have I've taken trades this year where I've held the position over the 5 PM settlement to 6
PM open. I think if I'm going to be able to put it probably on one hand, maybe five or less trades that I've
done that this year. I'm speaking in terms of real money, not demo. So I think that's the best way to be
doing it right now because of the inherent Gap risk that's always available. These things can pop off at any
time, send the market into some kind of crazy directional or whipsaw and upset of position that would
otherwise be profitable.

The low hanging fruit objective once we cleared this buy side, okay, was this level right here. I mentioned
this in last week's commentary before we went into Sunday's opening. I made it very clear, I do this every
single time, I do it on the weekend commentary. I've done it for years in private mentorship and no you can't
join it and no I don't make videos for them anymore, I'm teaching publicly for free, stop asking them send
me money, okay. Yeah, I don't want your money, I'm not selling any mentorships. And if anybody's
pretending to be me selling mentorships or videos they're scamming you, so don't do that.

But invariably you'll have folks that'll say, you know, 'but you said this and you said that.' In context I said
that because I don't know where we're going to open up for the week which was here on some of these
opening. I have to make concession that because we're going into non-farm payroll which is this week,
okay, that week. And I've taught this at nauseum through Twitter spaces. My long-term private mentorship
students are aware that we are under a protocol that I have taught them and it is basically try to make your
money.
NON
- FARM PAYROLL WEEK:

And this is for the folks that like to say I teach not to trade on Mondays, that's not true. Every non-farm
payroll week, okay, that means the economic number that comes out for non-frawal non-farm payroll
numbers or employment data that comes out typically but not always the first Friday of every month. That
week tends to be a little bit um problematic for symmetry meaning that after Wednesday, after Wednesday
it tends to be a little bit more difficult to have a one-sidedness to the marketplace where you can hold with
a great deal and I'm speaking in terms of the mindset, the skill set, the experience level of a new Trader.
Now clearly I can go out there in my my well-versed veteran students we can trade these days, okay. We
can we can trade a non-farm payroll too, you have done it, you've seen me do it, I've live streamed it, I've
called it, it's all there for people to try to say it's not there, go back and look at it's there.

I teach with a great deal of responsibility as a mentor and as an influencer. I don't want anyone that's new
to go out there thinking they could just walk out here and just try to trade this Market in this particular week
every single month because it's going to be problematic, you know, you don't have the experience to see
through some of the traps that are laid there before you. But over time you'll learn what they are and you
can look past when I tell the neophytes, the brand new students, I say don't trade, don't trade. But the folks
that have been with me for a while they knew their bottle, they're gonna trade and they're not going to listen
to that because they understand that the mentor and me, the teacher, is just guiding the new inexperienced
reader or student. If you already know what you're doing, obviously it goes without saying you're going to
do what you're going to do, you're not coming to me for insights. Many times you're probably not even
watching my stuff until after you've done whatever you're doing and you come back and say, 'okay let's see
what ICT said,' not because you're you're hanging on every word that I'm saying because you have your
own model now and that's how you graduate with me, that's how that's the goal, okay. There's no there's no
tethering for life with me, okay. There's no commitment to me for life, there's no key sending me money,
there's subscriptions to me, you play my bills please, that type of stuff, yeah, that's not necessary here,
okay. I want you to be an independent minded thinker and Trader and once you get your model you'll grow
past these training wheels. And these training wheels are given to you to protect you if you're a new
student, if you're a new Trader it's to remind you that there are real inherent risks to these markets
especially in the times where I tell you stay out. I'm speaking to the inexperienced Trader.

section_title: THE ELUSIVE WEEKEND OPENING & DAILY CHART OBJECTIVES
content:
So because we go into that new week with an unknown where the opening price is going to be at, it goes
without saying that obviously between where we were on the daily chart and we're dropping down down
into the daily. The old high we were aiming for two weeks ago, three weeks ago, four weeks ago, five weeks
ago always from back here, we got that and then it created a fair bag Gap in here. And I mentioned that
and I mentioned the wick in here consequent crushing that wick which it traded to beautifully and then
reacted off of that. Because of the weekend and not knowing where we're going to open up at, no one has
that crystal ball. I don't have that and I promise you I know some of you are going to think that I'm trying to
hold that back but if I did know it I'd be showing it off every single week, okay. Like I do everything else I
would show you. I promise you I would have done it and I would say you know this is I know this either I
would say hear it I can prove I can do it on this one I'm not going to teach it to you or I would probably just
teach it to you, okay. But I don't know, okay. And I spent a lot of time trying to figure out how to do that and
it just came to the conclusion that that's just one thing I don't need to know, okay. And while it would be
wonderful for me to have that and be able to have a conceptual idea on how to replicate that week after
week or at least a great deal of time, you know more times than not I don't have it, okay. And I don't believe
anybody ever will.

section_title: GAUGING WEEKEND SENTIMENT FROM MAINSTREAM MEDIA
content:
Because of the weekend sentiment, okay, where we close on Fridays for all markets, you have two days
where everybody sits on the weekend, they read their Barons, they read their Investors Business Daily,
their Wall Street Journal, you know whatever they're reading, okay, online forms and and whatnot. And
everybody has an opinion and they're trying to be the person that can say 'I told you so,' okay, 'I told you so,
I told you so.' And everybody's going to have a different varying opinion to some degree and it's why it's
important for you not to subscribe to people's opinion but using sentiment as a gauge on what you should
be weighing in terms of Street money or what would be considered retail thought, okay. So and when I look
at things like Investors Business Daily or if I look at Barons or Wall Street Journal and I have subscriptions
to those services, I like to see headlines that are very opinionated, very strong opinionated headlines. I
don't read the articles. Uh yeah, I don't really care what they have to say. It's what are they trying to get
everyone's attention around because that's going to build a sentiment idea that people that buy these
services think that they're doing them a favor by telling them what markets are going to go up or where
they're going to go down, what stocks to buy, what not, you know what stocks not to buy, okay. And it's
amazing how that herd mentality works.

And I go on social media, I read, you know, certain individuals that have live streams. I like reading their
chat, you know, seeing what they think collectively. In fact I go into some of them and encourage the hosts
and say, 'look can you do a poll?' Because if I don't get a good read on or if I show up to their chat late
because I'm doing other things, I may have missed a sentiment read on based on what they're chatting
about. So I'll ask them sometimes they don't see it or sometimes they see it, they just ignore it. But it's not a
knock against any one of these individual YouTubers, it's just the fact that they're chat window, their Live
Chat is just a wonderful resource for real retail sentiment and they're like a 90 losers club. And that doesn't
mean that they're stupid people, it just means that they're inexperienced but they're excited and I'm tapping
into their fevered pitch to get into the marketplace around a setting that's live around other people. And it's
it's the same way when you see a bunch of guys watch their favorite football team, they get Rowdy, you
know, and they're like, 'yeah,' and they can only see their team winning, they can't possibly imagine their
team losing. So when they subscribe to an idea that they think it's going to go up or down, the ones that are
on the fence they join that team mentality, that tribe mentality, which is a weakness as a Trader. You don't
want to have that. You want to be an apex predator, you want to be on the outside of the herd looking for
that weak one and then that's where the pack goes after and takes it down, the pack of smart money.

- STRATEGY & CONFIRMATION
On the weekend, I spend about an hour and I go through very static resources like like I said Barons which
is, you know, it's an resource where you can get, you know, opinions like a Wall Street Journal, um,
Investors Business Daily, looking at forexfactory.com. I look at general like Mainline mainstream media and
I see if there's any kind of, you know, headlines like Yahoo, okay. And believe me these people are not
mental Giants that write these articles and that's exactly why I'm tapping into them because they're using
their reach as influencers to try to inspire the investments of the neophyte, the people that just don't know.
And when you first start out as a Trader or an investor you think that these services are there to help you,
when they're not, they're just opinions, okay, they're just opinions. So on the weekend I spend a little bit of
time doing that very thing. So what I want to get in the beginning before we get to the opening sessions on
Monday morning, I already draw up an opinion about what I think the market is likely to perceive the new
coming week. In other words, the retail Traders collectively, did I see a lot of ideas or opinions or hard-line
people saying, 'there's no way we're definitely going down'? I love the seed dogmatic sentiment.
Sometimes it's not so dogmatic in the in the resources I tap into, but whatever it is that I come to it is
completely solidified when I tap into the live streamers that allow for their chat window to be open. So when
their little chat things go on that right there nails it down for me like it's absolutely 90 Club, you know, if you
fade that and your technicals are calling for something opposed to what they would be expecting now no no
take what I'm saying out of context I'm not saying I don't have an idea about wha
